landing.headline: Facing a housing problem? You are not alone.
landing.subheading: ClearPath India helps renters understand their rights, find government support, and know exactly what to do next.
landing.alreadyHaveAccount: Already have an account? Sign In
landing.step1Desc: Share your housing details in your language, privately and securely.
landing.step2Desc: Our system references local laws and guidelines to identify solutions.
landing.step3Desc: Follow a customized, step-by-step checklist to resolve your issue.
landing.footer: ClearPath India is not a law firm. All information is for guidance only.

auth.signUpSubtitle: Free forever. No unnecessary details asked.
auth.loginSubtitle: Sign in to continue your housing journey
auth.privacyNote: We store only your situation and action plan. We never share your data.
auth.haveAccount: Already have an account? Sign In
auth.noAccount: New here? Create a free account

intake.step: Step {{current}} of {{total}} — Tell us your situation
intake.welcome: Hello! I'm here to help you understand your housing situation. I'll ask you a few simple questions. There are no wrong answers — just tell me what's happening.
intake.q1: What best describes your current housing situation?
intake.q1Option5: Want to apply for government housing
intake.q2: Which city or state are you in?
intake.q3: Do you have a written rental agreement?
intake.summaryMsg: Got it. Let me look at what applies to your situation...
intake.bottomNote: You don't need to share your name or personal details.

proc.checkingLaws: We're checking relevant Indian housing laws...
proc.checkingSchemes: ...and government schemes that may apply to you.

results.title: Here's what we found for you
results.meansText: Based on your situation, you have specific legal protections. A landlord cannot evict you without following the proper legal process, which typically requires a written notice and a court order. Your rights vary depending on whether you have a written rental agreement.
results.disclaimer: This is based on general housing law. Your specific case may vary.
results.optionsPmayDesc: Apply for government affordable housing schemes. Check your eligibility based on household income.
results.optionsLegalAidDesc: Get free legal assistance from a government-appointed legal advisor under NALSA.
results.optionsComplaintDesc: Submit an official dispute to the local Rent Authority or Rent Tribunal.
results.optionsGuideDesc: Read our comprehensive guide outlining standard tenant rights and obligations.

help.banner: Your situation may need human support. Here's who can help.
help.card1Desc: Every district in India has a free legal aid centre under NALSA. You are entitled to free legal help by law.
help.card2Desc: Connect with verified housing support organisations like Shelter Associates, YUVA, and Jan Sahas.
help.card3Desc: You can approach your local Rent Authority to raise a formal complaint or dispute.
help.disclaimer: ClearPath India has helped you understand your situation. The next decision is yours. Please speak to a human advisor before taking any legal step.

dash.title: Welcome back.
dash.subtitle: Here's where things stand.
dash.emptyResources: No saved resources yet. Explore your results to save helpful links.
dash.modalTitle: Starting fresh will clear your current saved situation. Are you sure?
